title: Material-type CA & physics
description: Material registry with 16-bit tagged cells, hand-authored reaction rules, Margolus 2x2x2 movement phase, particles. Replaces the 3D Game of Life scaffolding.

title: 16-bit tagged cell encoding
description: Pack material-ID + metadata into 16 bits (expand to 32 if we hit the wall). Tagged-pointer style — Hashlife doesn't care about the split, it just memoizes state patterns.

title: Material registry with CPU+GPU lookup tables
description: Registry keyed by material ID returning properties (density, flammability, conductivity, color). Same table shipped to the GPU for shading. Start with room for 256, design for >256.

title: Reaction phase: hand-authored interaction table
description: 26-neighborhood pure CA for reactions: fire+wood->fire, water+lava->obsidian, temperature diffusion, signal propagation. Rules defined as data so they can be hot-authored.

title: Margolus 2x2x2 movement phase
description: Partition world into non-overlapping 2x2x2 blocks, apply permutation rules per block, alternate partition offset each generation. Mass-conserving because you rearrange rather than create/destroy.

title: Gravity and fluid flow via Margolus rules
description: Density-based swap rules: heavier-over-lighter swaps toward the heavier direction. Water spreads laterally with a bias. Sand falls. Lava flows slowly.

title: Particle support alongside CA
description: Out-of-grid particles for sparks, smoke, thrown objects. They live outside the octree state but interact with it at boundaries.

title: Fire/temperature/chemistry demo scene
description: Small showcase: burning wood room with water and lava, demonstrating reaction phase + movement phase together.

title: Terrain generation & infinite worlds
description: Multi-resolution noise-driven generation operating at octree-node granularity, cave smoothing, dungeons, infinite worlds via lazy root expansion.

title: Multi-resolution procedural gen(node_region) -> NodeId
description: Evaluate noise at region corners; if uniform, return uniform node without descending. Only subdivide where noise transitions occur (surface, caves, biome boundaries). Octree-native, not chunk-flat.

title: Cave smoothing CA pass (4-5 rule)
description: Classic 4-5 rule CA over the generated volume for organic cave shapes. Apply as a post-pass over generated nodes.

title: Dungeon carving layer
description: Rooms + corridors on top of base terrain. Procedural placement or WFC/graph grammars — pick the simpler one first.

title: Lazy root expansion for infinite worlds
description: When the player reaches the edge of the current root, wrap the root in a bigger parent whose other 7 children are default-terrain nodes, generated on demand.

title: Terrain-gen perf tracking
description: Per-region timing and memory accounting during generation. Flag if noise eval becomes the bottleneck; consider SIMD noise or CA-based gen as escape hatches.

title: SVDAG rendering
description: HashDAG-style SVDAG raycaster to replace the flat 3D-texture path at 1024^3+. Iterative GPU traversal (Laine-Karras lineage) with per-leaf material attributes and incremental edit uploads.

title: Serialize hash-consed DAG to GPU buffers
description: Contiguous layout for interior nodes (child pointers + occupancy mask) and leaf attributes. Upload as storage buffers. HashDAG-style so edits can be diff-uploaded.

comments.author: Edward Swernofsky
comments.text: Closed: serializer works. Confirmed end-to-end: World octree → Svdag::build → GPU storage buffer → WGSL raycaster → rendered pixels. Verified on 2488-voxel sphere scene: 418 nodes serialized, 15048 bytes, 6-level root, GoL3D sim evolving into 16k+ voxels, SVDAG rebuilding on each step (~1200 nodes).

title: Iterative SVDAG traversal shader
description: Laine-Karras lineage: iterative descent with position tracking. Start correctness-first, optimize later. Matches existing raycaster camera/orbit controls.

comments.author: Edward Swernofsky
comments.text: Closed: iterative DAG descent working. WGSL shader src/render/svdag_raycast.wgsl does stack-based iterative descent with pop-until-contains, descend-until-leaf, step-past-empty-octant pattern. Fixed one bug (see hash-thing-5eq) where pop-check EPS slack beat step-past advance, causing infinite loops on multi-axis boundary crossings. CPU replica (src/render/svdag.rs::cpu_trace) + 4 tests locked in as regression guards. Visual confirmation: SVDAG path renders the 2488-voxel sphere correctly, no scatter artifacts.

title: Runtime render-path toggle (flat <-> DAG)
description: Keyboard toggle between the existing flat 3D-texture raycaster and the new DAG raycaster. Keeps both paths working during development.
close_reason: V-key toggle implementation landed; SVDAG shader correctness tracked separately under 5bb.2 + 5eq bug

comments.author: Edward Swernofsky
comments.text: V-key toggle implemented in src/main.rs — presses 'V' to swap renderer.mode between Flat3D and Svdag. Build passes, runs on macOS. Closing.

title: Per-leaf material attributes in DAG
description: Extend DAG leaves to carry material IDs. Shader looks up material properties via the GPU material registry (from E2).

title: HashDAG-style incremental edit uploads
description: Upload only the nodes changed per simulation step, not the whole DAG. Embeds the DAG in a GPU hash table per Careil/Billeter/Eisemann 2020.

title: Investigate SSVDAG / sparse-64 / LOD streaming
description: Future optimization paths: symmetry-aware compression, 64-ary branching (~3x memory density), Aokana-style LOD streaming for 4096^3+. Deferred until baseline is stable.

title: SVDAG shader renders sparse scenes sparsely (bug)
description: SVDAG render path correctly draws dense regions (16^3 cube works) but only renders a fraction of filled voxels in sparse scenes. Test 1: 2488-cell random sphere shows only ~10-15 scattered voxels instead of dense surface. Test 2: 8 corner clusters (4^3 each, 512 voxels total) only 2 of 8 clusters visible. Hash-conses down to 6 unique nodes so serialization is correct. Needs investigation of the iterative descent/pop/step logic in svdag_raycast.wgsl — suspect step-past-empty octant or pop-until-contains logic skips filled sibling octants in some cases. Flat 3D path renders all three test cases correctly.

comments.author: Edward Swernofsky
comments.text: Strategy: writing a CPU-side Rust replica of the WGSL shader traversal logic in src/render/svdag.rs (as #[cfg(test)] module) so I can step through deterministically, bypassing the GPU black box. Will test against single-voxel, 4-voxel cluster, 8-corner, and random-sparse cases.

comments.author: Edward Swernofsky
comments.text:
Bug root cause: pop-check's EPS slack was larger than step-past's positional advance. When step-past advances t by +EPS, pos moves by |rd_axis| * EPS which is < EPS (e.g., 6.3e-6 for rd=0.631). The pop check uses pos_bound +/- EPS slack, so pos is still considered 'inside' the old octant after crossing into a sibling. Re-computing octant from the still-on-boundary position gives the same empty octant → infinite loop to MAX_STEPS.

Manifests on any ray that crosses a simultaneous two-axis boundary (e.g., x=y=0.5 plane) while one octant contains geometry and a sibling contains the target. Pure-diagonal rays (all three axes simultaneous) worked because the rays never needed to step-past.

Fix: strict pop check (no EPS slack). Step-past's +EPS on t still places pos geometrically past the boundary by |rd_axis|*EPS > 0, which strict bounds correctly detect.

Verified with CPU-side replica of shader traversal logic (src/render/svdag.rs cpu_trace module) reproducing the bug deterministically. Added 4 tests covering single-voxel, 8-corner cluster centers, 8-corner per-voxel exhaustive, and 300 random sparse voxels — all pass after fix.

title: Hashlife octree stepping
description: Recursive Hashlife-style step on the hash-consed octree, replacing the temporary brute-force grid stepper. Memoize step results per (NodeId, phase). This is load-bearing for all simulation performance.

title: Recursive Hashlife step on octree
description: Implement recursive step: at each level, combine 8 child results into the next NodeId. Start correctness-first using the existing brute-force stepper as reference. Direct octree operation, no flatten-to-grid.

title: Memoize step results by (NodeId, phase)
description: Add a step-result cache in NodeStore keyed on (NodeId, phase_or_rule). This is the core Hashlife performance win — identical subtrees compute their step exactly once.

title: Hashlife vs brute-force correctness harness
description: Property test: random seed -> N steps via brute-force grid == N steps via recursive Hashlife. Run across all four GoL presets and (later) material CA rules.

title: Margolus parity threaded through recursion
description: Phase 2 (movement) uses Margolus 2x2x2 blocks whose partition offset alternates by generation parity. Thread parity through the recursive step so memoization key is (NodeId, phase, parity).

title: Foundations & determinism
description: Cross-cutting: determinism audit (hash(pos,gen,seed) randomness, no global PRNG), perf measurement infra, replace Game of Life scaffolding.

title: hash(position, generation, seed) PRNG utility
description: Deterministic per-cell randomness. Required for probabilistic CA to remain Hashlife-compatible.

title: Determinism audit (no global PRNG, no scan order)
description: Walk the codebase, flag any global PRNG, thread_rng, or order-dependent iteration inside simulation or terrain-gen. Determinism is sacred.

title: Perf measurement infrastructure
description: Per-operation timing + memory counters for step, terrain gen, DAG serialization. Surface on-screen overlay or log at intervals.

title: Retire 3D Game of Life scaffolding
description: Once material CA + Margolus are in place, replace the four GoL presets and the brute-force grid stepper. Keep one GoL preset as a smoke test.

title: Cross-platform distribution
description: Native binaries for Mac/Linux/Windows via CI matrix, macOS notarization, AppImage, optional WASM web build, Steam deferred.

title: CI matrix: Mac + Linux + Windows builds
description: GitHub Actions matrix with cargo build --release for all three target triples. Cache target/ and cargo registry.

title: macOS notarization script
description: xcrun notarytool pipeline for distributing outside the App Store. Triggered on tagged releases.

title: Linux AppImage packaging
description: Single-file Linux distribution via AppImage. Bare binary as fallback.

title: Windows release .exe packaging
description: Bare .exe from the CI matrix; optional installer later.

title: WASM/WebGPU build config
description: wgpu -> WebGPU via WASM for shareable demos. Not the primary target but kept open.

title: Steam integration via steamworks-rs (deferred)
description: Thin layer added when there's a game to ship. Not a day-one concern, kept as a tracked line item.
